message:
test(refresh-ledger,bd-whnja): pin zero-change verdict under valid custom thresholds

Bead whnja flagged that RegressionVerdictThresholds::try_new accepted
negative warning values, letting a misconfigured CI bench turn a
steady-state (0%) delta into a Warning. Commit 5cb0038f (closes
3d7vo/93b7m, filed independently before whnja surfaced) already
landed the full fix: try_new rejects negative warning + negative
failure, the custom Deserialize validates too, regression_verdict
fail-opens Clean when a struct-update bypass produces an invalid
threshold pair. Tests covered the negative-rejection path and the
struct-update fail-open path.

What was still missing: a direct pin that a VALID non-default
threshold pair (common case — bench harnesses tune tolerance away
from the 15/30 defaults) evaluates a 0% steady-state delta as
Clean. Added:
  regression_verdict_zero_change_under_valid_custom_thresholds_is_clean
Exercises both a strict (5/20) and a loose (50/200) profile,
asserting Clean on Some(0.0) in both. A future refactor of the
`>= warning` / `>= failure` ordering can't silently flip no-op
runs into Warnings under a tight profile without tripping this.

Closes coding_agent_session_search-whnja.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
